name: MedLog_init_event
description: Initialize or overwrite a MedLog event record. Supply the 9-field MedLog payload to capture headers, inputs, identities, and initial artifacts.

header.description: MedLog header block including event_id, timestamps, risk metadata, and parent relationships.

model_instance.description: Model provenance metadata (model name, version, risk posture, vendor, etc.).

user_identity.description: Information about the requesting user, clinician, or agent.

target_identity.description: Optional target entity such as patient or device identifiers.

inputs.description: Structured input payload captured at initialization.

retention_tier.description: Retention tier label (steady, critical, transient, etc.).

name: MedLog_append_fragment
description: Append outputs, outcomes, artifacts, or feedback fragments to an existing MedLog event.

event_id.description: Identifier of the event to update.

fragment.description: Fragment payload containing any of internal_artifacts, outputs, outcomes, or user_feedback.

name: MedLog_get_provenance
description: Fetch PROV-JSON bundle for a given event to support audit trails and lineage review.

event_id.description: Identifier of the event to retrieve.

name: MedLog_query_events
description: Query MedLog events by run or event identifier. Useful for dashboarding, analytics, and sampling inspection.

run_id.description: Optional run identifier to filter results.

event_id.description: Optional event identifier to narrow results.

limit.description: Maximum number of rows to return (default 50).

name: MedLog_export_parquet
description: Trigger MedLog parquet export to the configured artifact directory.

name: MedLog_fhir_bundle
description: Retrieve the FHIR bundle synthesised for an individual MedLog event (Patient, Practitioner, Device, AuditEvent, Observations, Documents).

event_id.description: Identifier of the event to export.

name: MedLog_fhir_run_bundle
description: Aggregate all events in a run into a consolidated FHIR bundle for care-path review.
